edge_hypothesis: The advisory explicitly calls for testing extreme R:R variants (TP=10.0x, R:R=5:1) for tail-heavy distribution viability. The current MACD ACCEPT uses 1.0x stop / 3.5x TP (3.5:1 R:R) and achieves PF=1.347 with 189 trades. The hypothesis: MACD histogram zero-crosses occasionally catch massive multi-day crypto trends — these are the fat tails in the return distribution. By pushing R:R to 5:1 or higher, we accept a lower win rate but allow winners to grow 2-3x larger than the current ACCEPT's 3.5x TP cap. In crypto's regime-driven market structure, when MACD catches a regime transition (ranging-to-trending), the resulting move can easily exceed 7-10x ATR. The current 3.5x TP caps these winners prematurely. A single 7.5x ATR winner covers 5 stopped-out losses at 1.5x ATR, so we only need a 17% win rate to break even. The MACD ACCEPT already achieves ~33% win rate, so even with win rate degradation from wider TPs, there's substantial margin. This tests a fundamentally different profit distribution: few big winners vs many small winners. Regime assumption: ALL regimes — MACD works in ranging (PF=1.87) and trending (PF=1.58), both of which produce the large moves we want to capture. Transitional drag (PF=1.01) should improve because tight stops will cut transitional losers quickly while the few transitional winners that do develop get full room to run. Invalidation: if PF < 1.0 with extreme R:R, MACD zero-crosses do NOT catch fat tails — the moves exhaust before reaching 5x+ ATR, meaning crypto trends on 4h are shorter than the hypothesis requires.

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 5:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 7.5x TP). The core tail-harvesting variant. 1.5x ATR stop is above the mandatory safety floor and provides enough room to survive normal volatility. 7.5x ATR TP is 2.14x wider than the current ACCEPT — testing whether letting winners run to 7.5x ATR improves overall PF despite lower win rate. At 1.5x stop, this needs 17% win rate to break even (1.5 x 0.83 = 1.245 loss per loss-unit vs 7.5 x 0.17 = 1.275 gain per win-unit).
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0.0 to > 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0.0 to < 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 17% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis failure: if PF < 1.0, crypto 4h trends exhaust before 7.5x ATR
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend confirmation for MACD signal direction
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — extreme 5:1 R:R tail harvester

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_8to1_ultra
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R (1.0x stop / 8.0x TP). The ultra-patient variant. 1.0x ATR stop is tight — this deliberately accepts more stopped-out trades to keep losses small, banking everything on the rare trades that catch full 8.0x ATR moves. This is the most extreme R:R ever tested in the system. If MACD sometimes catches regime transitions that produce 8+ ATR moves, even a 11% win rate is profitable. The tight stop means we'll know quickly whether a signal is working or not.

entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0.0 to < 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bearish).

R:R = 8:1. Needs 11% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis failure: if win rate < 10%, even extreme R:R can't save the signal

Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_wide_stop
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 5:1 R:R (2.0x stop / 10.0x TP). The wide-breathing variant. Unlike the 1.5x and 1.0x stop variants, this uses 2.0x ATR stop — proven safe floor from advisory — giving the trade maximum room to survive initial noise after the MACD zero-cross. The 10.0x ATR TP is the widest ever tested, targeting major regime transitions where ETH moves 500+ pips on 4h. This variant will have the highest win rate of the three (wider stops = fewer stop-outs) but requires the biggest moves to hit TP. It tests whether ETH/4h produces 10x ATR moves frequently enough to be exploitable.

entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0.0 to < 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bearish).

R:R = 5:1. Needs 17% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis failure: if 0 trades hit 10.0x ATR TP, crypto 4h moves are bounded below 10x ATR
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
